name: Home overview dashboard unification across spending, budgets, and household activity
description: Rebuild the current lightweight home/overview page into the real top-level dashboard of the application. This is the broadest and highest-impact gap-closure feature because it needs to unify information that already exists across expenses, budgets, analytics, onboarding, and household management into one coherent first screen. The target outcome is a dashboard that helps users understand current financial state, take the next obvious action quickly, and navigate household context without hunting across separate pages. This work should stay intentionally integrative: reuse existing domain surfaces where possible, but raise the quality of the app's primary entry experience to match the maturity of the underlying feature set.
evidence: Completed frontend-first `/home` dashboard unification on 2026-05-08, then completed follow-ups on 2026-05-12 and 2026-05-13. The latest 2026-05-13 DataState pass renamed the shared state helper from `apps/web/src/components/shared/state-card.tsx` to `apps/web/src/components/shared/data-state.tsx`, exported `DataState`/`DataStateProps`, removed the success-state Card wrapper so successful children render directly, and updated Home widgets to own explicit Card anatomy when populated. Earlier 2026-05-13 card-composition evidence still applies: `apps/web/src/views/app/overview-page.tsx` uses shadcn Tabs for lens switching, `apps/web/src/components/home/lens-selector.tsx` was removed, and touched Home widgets use CardHeader/CardTitle/CardContent composition plus default Card, Button, Badge, Skeleton, and Progress primitives instead of custom visual overrides. The prior refinement evidence still applies for proactive household loading, reference-data-backed category presentation, and truthful dashboard behavior. Verification evidence: TDD red-green for `apps/web/src/components/home/home-card-composition.test.ts`, focused web Vitest command passed (18 files, 58 tests), `pnpm lint:fix` passed, `./init.sh`, and `gitnexus_detect_changes` (scope `all`) → low risk, 0 affected processes.
